perturbation.original_text: 10. Return of Information. Upon the written request of the Discloser, the Recipient shall either destroy or return to the Discloser any and all Confidential Information in the Recipient’s possession, except for one copy, which copy may be retained for archival purposes in a secure file. All copies of Confidential Information forwarded in error by any Discloser shall be destroyed in their entirety without the Recipient retaining an archival copy.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Return of Information. Upon the written request of the Discloser, the Recipient is permitted, but not required, to destroy or return to the Discloser any Confidential Information. The Recipient can keep any number of copies for any purpose. Copies of Confidential Information forwarded in error need not be destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict by making the return or destruction of confidential information optional rather than mandatory. The original clause requires the Recipient to either destroy or return all Confidential Information upon request, with a single exception for an archival copy. The modified text eliminates this obligation, stating that the Recipient is only 'permitted' to destroy or return the information, may keep copies without restriction and is not required to destroy information sent in error. This contradicts the spirit and purpose of the original confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Return of Information. Upon the written request of the Discloser, the Recipient is permitted, but not required, to destroy or return to the Discloser any Confidential Information. The Recipient can keep any number of copies for any purpose. Copies of Confidential Information forwarded in error need not be destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text makes the return or destruction of confidential information optional. Originally the Recipient shall either destroy or return to the Discloser any and all Confidential Information, but in the changed text they are not required. This change directly contradicts the idea of a confidentiality agreement, as the Recipient can keep any number of copies for any purpose. Therefore, the contradicted text is in direct conflict with the changed text, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12. No Assignment. Neither Party shall transfer or assign any rights or delegate any ob- ligations herein, in whole or in part, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, without the prior written consent of the other Party, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Notwithstanding the above, either Party shall have the right to assign this Agreement and the obligations hereunder to any successor-in-interest by way of a merger, acquisi- tion, or sale of all or substantially all of that Party’s assets on condition that the assign-ee of this Agreement will assume all obligations and liability of the assignor under or in connection with this Agreement and agrees to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. No Assignment. Either Party may assign this Agreement to any third party without notice or consent from the other Party. The assigning Party shall not be responsible for informing the other party of the assignment.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction concerning assignment. The original clause prohibits assignment without the other party's consent (not to be unreasonably withheld) but allows assignment in case of merger or acquisition if the assignee assumes all obligations. The new clause allows assignment to any third party without any notice or consent, and removes the assignor's responsibility to inform the other party. This completely contradicts the original requirement for consent and the conditions for assignment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. No Assignment. Either Party may assign this Agreement to any third party without notice or consent from the other Party. The assigning Party shall not be responsible for informing the other party of the assignment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text completely contradicts the original text, as it allows assignment to any third party without notice or consent, while the original required consent (not to be unreasonably withheld) and had specific conditions for assignment in case of merger or acquisition. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and thus the Change Text contradicts the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Entire Agreement. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties with respect to this subject matter, is valid if signed within forty-five (45) days after the Effective Date, and supersedes all prior understandings whether written or oral. Further modifications may be made if in writing and signed by authorized repre- sentatives of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Entire Agreement. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties with respect to this subject matter and supersedes all prior understandings whether written or oral. This agreement is valid regardless of the date signed after the Effective Date. Further modifications may be made orally and are binding whether or not they are signed.
perturbation.explanation: This creates a contradiction in terms of how the contract can be modified. The original says modifications must be in writing and signed, while the changed text says oral modifications are binding. It also removes the validity period after the effective date creating further ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. Entire Agreement. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties with respect to this subject matter and supersedes all prior understandings whether written or oral. This agreement is valid regardless of the date signed after the Effective Date. Further modifications may be made orally and are binding whether or not they are signed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 14 creates a direct contradiction. The original text states that modifications must be in writing and signed, whereas the changed text states that oral modifications are binding whether or not they are signed. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text which contains the new contradictory information. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding how the contract can be modified, making it an in-text contradiction as per the definition provided.
